overall.summary: 4 categories are live and 2 are partial. 2 source groups are unavailable.

categories.name: Economy
categories.summary: Mixed movement across prices, labor, credit, and consumer activity.
categories.changed: Consumer pressure increased modestly while labor indicators remained comparatively stable.
categories.matters: The combination can affect household spending before it becomes visible in headline economic data.
categories.confidence: Moderate — several measures agree, but the direction is not uniform.

categories.name: Supply Chains
categories.summary: Transportation and inventory pressure are above the recent baseline.
categories.changed: Selected freight, delivery-time, and inventory measures moved higher.
categories.matters: Persistent pressure can reach stores and manufacturers through delays or higher costs.
categories.confidence: Moderate — movement is measurable but not yet broad-based.

categories.name: Infrastructure
categories.summary: Most monitored systems remain within normal operating ranges.
categories.changed: Localized interruptions rose, while national-level reliability remained stable.
categories.matters: Current changes appear manageable unless disruptions begin clustering across regions.
categories.confidence: High — the majority of monitored indicators remain aligned.

sourceDetails.name: National Weather Service active alerts
sourceDetails.detail: Weather component: 510 active alerts; 119 severe or extreme.

categories.name: Conflict
categories.summary: International activity remains elevated and deserves continued attention.
categories.changed: Operational tempo and diplomatic strain are above their recent baseline.
categories.matters: Escalation can affect energy, transport, markets, and public confidence even without direct domestic impact.
categories.confidence: Moderate — activity is clear, but future direction is uncertain.

categories.name: Energy
categories.summary: Prices and supply conditions remain uneven but manageable.
categories.changed: Short-term price volatility increased while physical supply stayed broadly available.
categories.matters: Volatility matters most when it persists long enough to affect household and business costs.
categories.confidence: Moderate-high — pricing data is clear; downstream impact is less certain.

categories.name: Public Safety
categories.summary: No broad national shift is currently visible.
categories.changed: Some regional measures rose, but the national pattern remains mixed.
categories.matters: Regional movement matters locally without necessarily indicating a wider trend.
categories.confidence: Moderate — reporting timing varies across jurisdictions.

sourceDetails.name: National Weather Service active alerts
sourceDetails.detail: Weather component: 510 active alerts; 119 severe or extreme.

categories.name: Weather
categories.summary: National weather pressure is calculated from active official alerts.
categories.changed: There are 510 active actual alerts, including 119 severe or extreme alerts.
categories.matters: Widespread or severe weather can affect travel, utilities, emergency response, and household safety.
categories.confidence: Moderate-high — direct NWS feed; alert counts do not measure realized damage.

sourceDetails.name: National Weather Service active alerts
sourceDetails.detail: 510 active actual alerts; 119 severe or extreme.

categories.name: Earthquakes
categories.summary: Seismic pressure reflects significant earthquakes reported during the past week.
categories.changed: USGS reports 3 significant earthquakes; the largest magnitude is 7.3.
categories.matters: Significant earthquakes can create localized life-safety, infrastructure, and supply-chain consequences.
categories.confidence: High — direct USGS event feed; the national score does not estimate local damage.

categories.name: Cybersecurity
categories.summary: Cyber pressure reflects new vulnerabilities confirmed as exploited in the wild.
categories.changed: CISA added 9 known exploited vulnerabilities in seven days and 30 in 30 days.
categories.matters: Newly exploited vulnerabilities increase patching urgency for affected organizations, but do not mean every user is exposed.
categories.confidence: High for catalog additions — CISA is authoritative; the score is not a measure of all cyber incidents.

sourceDetails.name: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
sourceDetails.detail: 9 additions in seven days; 30 in 30 days; 0 marked known ransomware use.

categories.name: Space Weather
categories.summary: Space-weather pressure uses recent NOAA SWPC alert products.
categories.changed: NOAA SWPC issued 12 alert products during the past seven days.
categories.matters: Strong geomagnetic and solar activity can affect radio, navigation, satellites, and some power-grid operations.
categories.confidence: Moderate-high — official NOAA feed; product counts are a proxy for operational severity.
sources: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center alerts

sourceDetails.name: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center alerts
sourceDetails.detail: 12 products issued in seven days; 8 matched alert or warning language.

changes.title: International activity remains above baseline
changes.detail: The signal is elevated, but the evidence does not establish a clear next direction.

changes.title: Freight pressure increased modestly
changes.detail: The change is measurable and worth monitoring, though not yet severe.

changes.title: Short-term price volatility increased
changes.detail: Physical availability remains broadly stable, limiting immediate practical impact.

limitations: Scores are risk indicators, not forecasts.
Missing sources reduce confidence rather than being silently replaced.
Some categories use public proxy indicators because no single authoritative measure exists.

intelligence.title: Cybersecurity is the leading measurable driver
intelligence.summary: Broadly stable. 2 evidence-backed drivers account for the most meaningful movement in this refresh. The strongest measured change is cybersecurity, which fell sharply.

drivers.explanation: Cybersecurity fell sharply to 90/100. CISA added 9 known exploited vulnerabilities in seven days and 30 in 30 days.
Weather edged lower to 98/100. There are 510 active actual alerts, including 119 severe or extreme alerts.
